== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]]{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)}} [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Shrine |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]]
[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

[[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]]
[[category:qq close]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[Category:New uncategorized original categories]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

[[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

[[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

[[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[Category:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] [[ja:若宮八幡神社_(佐久市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]
{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Saku}}
