{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|12=draft}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|12=draft}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=draft}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=draft}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅|12=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]
{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Saku}}

25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市) up to rev 98258602
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[佐久市]]大字[[岩村田]]1907<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=若宮神社 |publisher=佐久市 |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 11.37 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 28|経度秒 = 25.21 |祭神 = [[仁徳天皇|大雀命]]、[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]、[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]]、[[武内宿禰]]<ref name="kitasaku">『北佐久郡志』[{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} 第四編 町村篇 88ページ]。</ref> |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[郷社]]<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=郷土探訪 郷社若宮八幡神社（佐久市岩村田） |publisher=佐久浅間農業協同組合 |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |創建 = [[建仁]]2年（[[1202年]]）<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]]<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |別名 = 若宮神社<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月22日]]<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |ラベル位置 = left }} '''若宮八幡神社''' (わかみやはちまんじんじゃ) は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]][[岩村田]]にある[[神社]]。'''若宮神社'''とも<ref name="saku_jinja" />。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
== 祭神 == [[祭神]]は[[仁徳天皇|大雀命]]、[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]、[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]]、[[武内宿禰]]<ref name="kitasaku" />。
岩村田、平塚の[[産土神]]<ref name="kitasaku" />。
== 歴史 == * [[建仁]]2年（[[1202年]]、[[鎌倉時代]]） - 当地を治めていた[[大井朝光]]が、[[鎌倉]]の[[鶴岡八幡宮]]（神奈川県[[鎌倉市]]）より[[分霊|分祀・勧請]]して創建したという<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * [[応永]]5年（1398年、[[室町時代]]） - [[伝染病]]（[[疫病]]）の流行を鎮めるため、[[尾張国]]の[[津島神社]]（愛知県[[津島市]]）より[[祇園信仰|祇園]]の神を勧請<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=信州佐久 旅の観光ガイド 岩村田祇園祭 |publisher=長野県佐久市観光協会 |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref>。 * 応永20年（1413年） - [[大石氏]]より[[懸額]]と[[鈴|古鈴]]が[[寄進]]、奉納<ref name="saku_jinja" />。 * [[元禄]]4年（1691年、[[江戸時代]]） - 現在の[[神社建築|本殿]]を建立<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
[[本陣]]・[[脇本陣]]としての役割も有していた<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
== 境内 == * 本殿 - 元禄4年（1691年、江戸時代）建立。総[[ケヤキ|欅材]]による[[流造|三間社流造]]<ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * [[境内社]] - [[牛頭天王|祇園牛頭天王]]（[[岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|岩村田祇園祭]]の神）を祀る<ref name="jasakuasama" />。[[神輿]]は佐久市指定有形文化財（岩村田若宮神社祇園社神輿）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/bunkazaijimusyo/shiteibunkazai.html |title=指定文化財一覧 |publisher=佐久市 |date=2017-06-05 |accessdate=2017-06-18 }}</ref>。『北佐久郡志』（1915年）によると社名を「速進雄社」とし、祭神は[[スサノオ|速進雄命]]、[[タキリビメ|多紀理比売命]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵比売命]]、[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]]、[[タギツヒメ|多岐津比売命]]、[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]]、[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]、[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]]、[[菅原道真|菅原道真朝臣]]で、大井朝光の子孫が応永年間に尾張国の津島神社から移したとある<ref name="kitasaku" />。 * [[常夜灯]] - [[中山道]]・[[岩村田宿]]にあったものを移設<ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * 若宮公園 - 社殿の北にある公園。[[面積]]3,600[[平方メートル]]。[[春]]は[[サクラ|桜]]が見事で、[[巨樹]]が多く[[夏]]も涼やかである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=若宮公園 |publisher=佐久市 |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>。
== 祭事 == * [[7月]]中旬 - [[岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|岩村田祇園祭]]<ref name="gion" /> * [[8月22日]] - [[秋]]の[[例祭]]。[[農作物]]の収穫を前に[[豊作|五穀豊穣]]・家内安全・[[交通安全]]を願う<ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * [[相撲]]大会 - 江戸時代からの伝統行事<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=若宮神社 子ども奉納相撲 豆力士70人が取り組み |publisher=小諸新聞 |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐久平駅]]から[[自動車]]で約10分間<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『[{{NDLDC|1877657}} 北佐久郡志]』長野県北佐久郡役所、1915年。
== 関連項目 == * [[信濃国]] - [[佐久郡]] - [[北佐久郡]] - [[岩村田町]] * [[大井氏]] * [[大井城]] * [[岩村田藩]] * [[岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り]] - [[祇園祭]] - [[祇園信仰]] - [[津島信仰]] * [[八幡神]] - [[八幡宮]] - [[若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] * [[若宮八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA佐久浅間 郷土探訪 郷社若宮八幡神社（佐久市岩村田）] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html 若宮神社] - [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html 若宮公園] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ 信州佐久 旅の観光ガイド 岩村田祇園祭]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] to [[Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku, Nagano)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[長野県]][[佐久市]]大字[[岩村田]]1907<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=若宮神社 |publisher=佐久市 |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 16|緯度秒 = 11.37 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 28|経度秒 = 25.21 |祭神 = [[仁徳天皇|大雀命]]、[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]、[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]]、[[武内宿禰]]<ref name="kitasaku">『北佐久郡志』[{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} 第四編 町村篇 88ページ]。</ref> |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[郷社]]<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=郷土探訪 郷社若宮八幡神社（佐久市岩村田） |publisher=佐久浅間農業協同組合 |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |創建 = [[建仁]]2年（[[1202年]]）<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]]<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |別名 = 若宮神社<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |札所等 = |例祭 = [[8月22日]]<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nagano |ラベル位置 = left }} '''若宮八幡神社''' (わかみやはちまんじんじゃ) は、[[長野県]][[佐久市]][[岩村田]]にある[[神社]]。'''若宮神社'''とも<ref name="saku_jinja" />。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
== 祭神 == [[祭神]]は[[仁徳天皇|大雀命]]、[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]、[[タケミナカタ|建御名方命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]]、[[武内宿禰]]<ref name="kitasaku" />。
岩村田、平塚の[[産土神]]<ref name="kitasaku" />。
== 歴史 == * [[建仁]]2年（[[1202年]]、[[鎌倉時代]]） - 当地を治めていた[[大井朝光]]が、[[鎌倉]]の[[鶴岡八幡宮]]（神奈川県[[鎌倉市]]）より[[分霊|分祀・勧請]]して創建したという<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * [[応永]]5年（1398年、[[室町時代]]） - [[伝染病]]（[[疫病]]）の流行を鎮めるため、[[尾張国]]の[[津島神社]]（愛知県[[津島市]]）より[[祇園信仰|祇園]]の神を勧請<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=信州佐久 旅の観光ガイド 岩村田祇園祭 |publisher=長野県佐久市観光協会 |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref>。 * 応永20年（1413年） - [[大石氏]]より[[懸額]]と[[鈴|古鈴]]が[[寄進]]、奉納<ref name="saku_jinja" />。 * [[元禄]]4年（1691年、[[江戸時代]]） - 現在の[[神社建築|本殿]]を建立<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
[[本陣]]・[[脇本陣]]としての役割も有していた<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
== 境内 == * 本殿 - 元禄4年（1691年、江戸時代）建立。総[[ケヤキ|欅材]]による[[流造|三間社流造]]<ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * [[境内社]] - [[牛頭天王|祇園牛頭天王]]（[[岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|岩村田祇園祭]]の神）を祀る<ref name="jasakuasama" />。[[神輿]]は佐久市指定有形文化財（岩村田若宮神社祇園社神輿）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/bunkazaijimusyo/shiteibunkazai.html |title=指定文化財一覧 |publisher=佐久市 |date=2017-06-05 |accessdate=2017-06-18 }}</ref>。『北佐久郡志』（1915年）によると社名を「速進雄社」とし、祭神は[[スサノオ|速進雄命]]、[[タキリビメ|多紀理比売命]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵比売命]]、[[アマツヒコネ|天津日子根命]]、[[タギツヒメ|多岐津比売命]]、[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳命]]、[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]、[[クマノクスビ|熊野久須毘命]]、[[菅原道真|菅原道真朝臣]]で、大井朝光の子孫が応永年間に尾張国の津島神社から移したとある<ref name="kitasaku" />。 * [[常夜灯]] - [[中山道]]・[[岩村田宿]]にあったものを移設<ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * 若宮公園 - 社殿の北にある公園。[[面積]]3,600[[平方メートル]]。[[春]]は[[サクラ|桜]]が見事で、[[巨樹]]が多く[[夏]]も涼やかである<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=若宮公園 |publisher=佐久市 |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>。
== 祭事 == * [[7月]]中旬 - [[岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|岩村田祇園祭]]<ref name="gion" /> * [[8月22日]] - [[秋]]の[[例祭]]。[[農作物]]の収穫を前に[[豊作|五穀豊穣]]・家内安全・[[交通安全]]を願う<ref name="jasakuasama" />。 * [[相撲]]大会 - 江戸時代からの伝統行事<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=若宮神社 子ども奉納相撲 豆力士70人が取り組み |publisher=小諸新聞 |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>。
== 交通アクセス == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐久平駅]]から[[自動車]]で約10分間<ref name="jasakuasama" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『[{{NDLDC|1877657}} 北佐久郡志]』長野県北佐久郡役所、1915年。
== 関連項目 == * [[信濃国]] - [[佐久郡]] - [[北佐久郡]] - [[岩村田町]] * [[大井氏]] * [[大井城]] * [[岩村田藩]] * [[岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り]] - [[祇園祭]] - [[祇園信仰]] - [[津島信仰]] * [[八幡神]] - [[八幡宮]] - [[若宮八幡宮 (曖昧さ回避)]] * [[若宮八幡神社 (曖昧さ回避)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA佐久浅間 郷土探訪 郷社若宮八幡神社（佐久市岩村田）] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html 若宮神社] - [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html 若宮公園] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ 信州佐久 旅の観光ガイド 岩村田祇園祭]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gōsha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (village shrine).

The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.

The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />

== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Saku City|ja|佐久市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saku City|qq=}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|ja|鶴岡八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|qq=}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|ja|津島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsushima Shrine|qq=}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|ja|佐久平駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakudaira Station|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]
{{translated page|ja|若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)|version=98258602|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (village shrine).

== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg|250px]] |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|qid=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).

The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|qid=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|qid=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]
