<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|qid=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).

The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.

The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]
{{translated page|ja|若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)|version=98258602|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]
{{translated page|ja|若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)|version=98258602|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ([[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]]) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from [[:en:Tsushima Shrine|Tsushima Shrine]] in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s [[:en:Sakudaira Station|Sakudaira Station]].<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (佐久市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22120889]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|en|Sakudaira Station|WD=Q1188565|lt=Sakudaira Station}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|en|Sakudaira Station|WD=Q1188565|lt=Sakudaira Station}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821|1=Saku, Nagano}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City|1=Saku, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|en|Sakudaira Station|WD=Q1188565|lt=Sakudaira Station|1=Sakudaira Station}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:若宮八幡神社（長野県佐久市） 01.jpg |location = 1907 Iwamurada, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture, Japan<ref name="saku_jinja">{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html |title=Wakamiya Shrine |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref> |ISO = JP-20 |Latitude = 36|LatMin = 16|LatSec = 11.37 |Longitude = 138|LongMin = 28|LongSec = 25.21 |Deities = Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto), Takeminakata, Kotoshironushi, Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku">"Kitazaku District Records" [{{NDLDC|1877657/448}} Part 4, Town Section, p. 88].</ref> |MainHall = Three-bay nagare-zukuri (flowing style)<ref name="jasakuasama">{{Cite web|url=http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php |title=Local Exploration: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada) |publisher=JA Saku Asama Agricultural Cooperative |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> |Rank = Former Gō-sha (Village Shrine)<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Foundation = Ken'nin 2 (1202)<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> |Alias = Wakamiya Shrine<ref name="saku_jinja" /> |Festival = August 22<ref name="jasakuasama" /> |prefecture= {{ill|Saku|WD=Q495821|1=Saku, Nagano}} |coordinates= {{Coord|36.269825|N|138.473669|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社, Wakamiya Hachiman Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City|1=Saku, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. Also referred to as '''Wakamiya Shrine'''<ref name="saku_jinja" />, it is a {{ill|Former Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (village shrine).
== Deities == The enshrined deities include: * Emperor Ōjin ({{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}) * Emperor Nintoku (Ōsazaki no Mikoto) * Takeminakata * Kotoshironushi * Takenouchi no Sukune<ref name="kitasaku" />
The shrine serves as the tutelary deity (ubusuna no kami) of Iwamurada and Hiratsuka<ref name="kitasaku" />.
== History == * Ken'nin 2 (1202) - consecration_year by Oi Tomomitsu, the local ruler, who enshrined deities from {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} in Kamakura.<ref name="saku_jinja" /><ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Ōei 5 (1398) - Deities associated with Gion belief were added from {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|WD=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine|1=Tsushima Shrine}} in Owari Province to quell an epidemic.<ref name="gion">{{Cite web|url=http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ |title=Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival |publisher=Saku City Tourism Association |accessdate=2017-06-19 }}</ref> * Ōei 20 (1413) - The Ōishi clan donated plaques and bells.<ref name="saku_jinja" /> * Genroku 4 (1691) - Current main sanctuary constructed using keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
The shrine also served as an important post station on the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== Grounds == * Main Sanctuary - Built in Genroku 4 (1691), the structure features a flowing nagare-zukuri style with keyaki wood.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Auxiliary Shrine - Dedicated to Gion Gyōten Tenno, the deity celebrated during the {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Stone Lantern - Relocated from the Nakasendō route.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Wakamiya Park - Located north of the shrine, this park spans 3,600 square meters and is known for cherry blossoms and ancient trees.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/shisetsu/undo_koen_hoyo/koen/saku/wakamiya.html |title=Wakamiya Park |publisher=Saku City |date=2015-02-02 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Festivals == * Mid-July - {{ill|Iwamurada Gion Festival|ja|岩村田祇園祭お船様祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwamurada Gion Festival|qq=}}<ref name="gion" /> * August 22 - Autumn Festival, praying for a good harvest, family safety, and traffic safety.<ref name="jasakuasama" /> * Sumo Matches - Traditional event dating back to the Edo period.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://komoronews.co.jp/2017/05/01/%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E5%AD%90%E3%81%A9%E3%82%82%E5%A5%89%E7%B4%8D%E7%9B%B8%E6%92%B2%E3%80%80%E8%B1%86%E5%8A%9B%E5%A3%AB70%E4%BA%BA%E3%81%8C%E5%8F%96%E3%82%8A%E7%B5%84%E3%81%BF/ |title=Wakamiya Shrine Children's Sumo Matches |publisher=Komoro News |date=2017-05-01 |accessdate=2017-06-17 }}</ref>
== Access == * 10-minute drive from {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道株式会社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}'s {{ill|Sakudaira Station|en|Sakudaira Station|WD=Q1188565|lt=Sakudaira Station|1=Sakudaira Station}}.<ref name="jasakuasama" />
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.ja-sakuasama.iijan.or.jp/welcomenews/monthly/play/2013/09/post_122.php JA Saku Asama: Local Exploration - Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine (Saku City, Iwamurada)] * [https://www.city.saku.nagano.jp/kanko/spot/nakasendo/iwamuradashuku/wakamiyajinja.html Wakamiya Shrine] * [http://www.sakukankou.jp/event/iwamurada-gion/ Shinshu Saku Travel Guide: Iwamurada Gion Festival]
